title: Assertion when calling $clog2
body:
Calling
```
iverilog -g2012 -o test test.sv
```
with this `test.sv`:
```
module test;
	function void fun;
		$clog2(1);
	endfunction
	always_comb fun;
endmodule
```
fails with an assertion:
```
test.sv:5: warning: always_comb process has no sensitivities.
ivl: net_scope.cc:502: const NetTaskDef* NetScope::task_def() const: Assertion `type_ == TASK' failed.
sh: line 1: 11118 Done                    /usr/local/lib/ivl/ivlpp -L -F"/tmp/ivrlg25dedf82f" -f"/tmp/ivrlg5dedf82f" -p"/tmp/ivrli5dedf82f"
     11119 Aborted                 | /usr/local/lib/ivl/ivl -C"/tmp/ivrlh5dedf82f" -C"/usr/local/lib/ivl/vvp.conf" -- -
```

Replacing `always_comb` with `always` or `initial` works fine. Calling `$clog2` within an `always_comb` block directly (without being wrapped in another function) also works. It prints this warning though:
```
warning: System task ($clog2) cannot be synthesized in an always_comb process.
```
Is this true? Shouldn't this get optimized away during synthesis when the argument to `$clog2` is constant at compile time?
code_blocks:
test.sv:5: warning: always_comb process has no sensitivities.
ivl: net_scope.cc:502: const NetTaskDef* NetScope::task_def() const: Assertion `type_ == TASK' failed.
sh: line 1: 11118 Done                    /usr/local/lib/ivl/ivlpp -L -F"/tmp/ivrlg25dedf82f" -f"/tmp/ivrlg5dedf82f" -p"/tmp/ivrli5dedf82f"
     11119 Aborted                 | /usr/local/lib/ivl/ivl -C"/tmp/ivrlh5dedf82f" -C"/usr/local/lib/ivl/vvp.conf" -- -
warning: System task ($clog2) cannot be synthesized in an always_comb process.
